channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Faible] UK investigates TikTok for alleged age-verification lapses, exposing kids to online harms
item.description: “Age checks are a cornerstone of the UK’s online safety laws,” said Ofcom’s Chief Executive, Melanie Dawes. “Too many services have no or inadequate age checks in place, which is not good enough.”

item.title: [Faible] Ukrainians rally against dismissal of tech-minded defense minister Fedorov
item.description: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky dismissed Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, who championed the push to integrate drone technology and digital innovation into the military.

item.title: [Critique] ThreatsDay: Game Cheat Spyware, 24-Hour Ransomware, Chrome Sync Stalking + 12 More Stories
item.description:
A lot of this week’s trouble starts with something that looks close enough.
A familiar repo. A useful installer. A harmless sync setting. Then the handoff goes bad, the box starts talking to someone else, and the…

item.title: [Faible] AI Agents Broke the Security Playbook. Here's What Replaces It.
item.description: Traditional security workflows were built for environments that changed at human speed. Token Security explains why AI agents require a new approach: building on a live identity foundation while giving security teams…

item.title: [Faible] Sandworm hackers have a CAPTCHA trick for Ukrainians
item.description: Rather than verifying they are human, the CAPTCHA users are instructed to copy and paste a PowerShell command into their Windows computers.

item.title: [Élevé] 23andMe to pay $18 million in new genetics data breach settlement
item.description: Genetic testing company 23andMe has agreed to pay $18 million to settle claims from a coalition of 43 attorneys general that it failed to protect customers' genetic data. [...]

item.title: [Faible] n8n Token Exchange Flaw Could Let Attackers Log In as Users From Another Issuer
item.description: n8n, the workflow automation platform, handed out the wrong accounts at login. On Enterprise instances configured to trust more than one external token issuer, it matched an incoming JWT to a local user on…

item.title: [Faible] New TELEPUZ Malware Spreads via ClickFix to Steal Data and Run Commands
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have called attention to a new modular malware called TELEPUZ that's been spreading via websites infected with ClickFix lures since late April 2026.
"The malware is full-featured, lightweight,…

item.title: [Faible] New ClickLock macOS Stealer Kills Apps Every 210ms Until Victims Type Their Password
item.description: ClickLock Stealer, a new macOS infostealer, answers a victim's refusal by killing their apps on a loop until they hand over the login password. It arrives as a command pasted into Terminal, asks for the password behind…

item.title: [Faible] Scattered Spider members behind TfL hack get five years in prison
item.description: Two leading members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime collective were sentenced to five years and six months in prison each for hacking Transport for London (TfL) in 2024. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Scattered Spider hackers sentenced to 5.5 years over £29 million Transport for London hack
item.description: Two leading members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime collective have been sentenced to more than five years in prison for carrying out the 2024 cyberattack against Transport for London (TfL).

item.title: [Faible] Windows 11 24H2 Home and Pro reach end of support in 90 days
item.description: Microsoft announced on Wednesday that systems running Windows 10 Enterprise LTSB 2016 and Home and Pro editions of Windows 11 24H2 will stop receiving updates in three months. [...]

item.title: [Faible] 20+ Hijacked Government Websites Became an Attack Channel
item.description: More than 20 Brazilian government websites were hijacked and turned into malware delivery channels in an active PhantomEnigma campaign uncovered by ANY.RUN, a leading provider of interactive malware analysis and threat…

item.title: [Faible] New Agent Data Injection Attack Can Make AI Agents Misclick or Run Attacker Commands
item.description: Ask an AI agent to summarize the reviews on a product page, and a single planted review can make it click "Buy Now" instead. Ask a coding assistant to apply a maintainer's fix from a GitHub thread, and a fake comment…

item.title: [Faible] Daxin Resurfaces in Taiwan Alongside Stupig Pre-Login SYSTEM Backdoor
item.description:
An advanced malware previously attributed to a China-linked threat actor has resurfaced after more than four years within a Taiwan manufacturing firm, along with a previously unreported backdoor dubbed Stupig.
Daxin…

item.title: [Critique] CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Oracle flaw by Saturday
item.description: CISA has ordered federal agencies to secure their systems by Saturday against ongoing attacks exploiting a critical vulnerability in the Oracle E-Business Suite financial application. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Russian hackers trojanize WebEx, Zoom apps to push Starland malware
item.description: A financially motivated Russian threat actor tracked as UAT-11795 is using trojanized software to steal credentials and cryptocurrency by deploying a new backdoor called Starland RAT. [...]

item.title: [Faible] AI Can Find Bugs, But Human Knowledge Still Proves Them
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is changing offensive security, but it has not changed the standard that matters most: a finding has to be proven before it becomes useful. AI-assisted tools can read code quickly, generate…

item.title: [Critique] New Spirals ransomware encrypts victim network in under 24 hours
item.description: A new ransomware actor called Spirals completed a corporate intrusion, from initial access to data theft and encryption, in less than 24 hours. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] Unpatched Shark Vacuum Flaw Could Let Attackers Control Other Vacuums Region-Wide
item.description: Pull the certificate off the flash of a Shark RV2320EDUS robot vacuum, and you can run root commands on other people's Shark vacuums across the same AWS region: watch the camera, drive the robot, read the map of the…

item.title: [Moyen] OpenAI’s GPT-Red Automates Prompt Injection Testing to Harden GPT-5.6 Sol
item.description:
OpenAI has disclosed details of GPT-Red, an internal automated red-teaming model that scales prompt injection vulnerability discovery with an aim to fix issues before the tools are deployed widely.
"GPT‑Red is a strong…

item.title: [Moyen] Zoom Patches Critical Windows Flaw That Could Enable Account Takeover
item.description:
Zoom has released security updates for a critical security flaw impacting Zoom Workplace for Windows that could facilitate account takeover.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-53412 (CVSS score: 9.8), affects Zoom…

item.title: [Faible] Cyberattack on Japan's largest cold-chain operator disrupts KFC, supermarket supplies
item.description: Kentucky Fried Chicken restaurants have been left short on ingredients and major restaurant chains struggling to keep up with deliveries following a cyberattack on Nichirei Logistics Group.

item.title: [Faible] Dutch police bust investment fraud ring stealing over €100 million
item.description: The Dutch Police announced the arrest of multiple individuals suspected of being part of an international investment fraud scheme estimated to have tens of thousands of victims. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Zoom warns of critical account takeover vulnerability
item.description: Zoom is warning of a critical vulnerability in its desktop client and software development kit for Windows that could be exploited by an unauthenticated party to hijack accounts. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] Trump administration unveils AI-supported clearinghouse for cyber vulnerabilities
item.description: The Gold Eagle program will allowe industry, critical infrastructure operators and the government to use artificial intelligence to rapidly detect, prioritize and patch cybersecurity vulnerabilities, officials said.

item.title: [Faible] TuxBot v3 Evolution Shows Signs of LLM-Assisted IoT Botnet Development
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a previously unreported Internet-of-Things (IoT) botnet framework dubbed TuxBot v3 Evolution that shows signs of being developed with assistance from a large language…

item.title: [Critique] Google Gemini CLI abused as a hacking agent, malware botnet operator
item.description: A Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" used Google's open-source Gemini CLI AI tool as a hacking agent and to operate a small-scale botnet. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Kosc Telecom : près de 4 000 diagnostics et accès fibre dans une fuite de données
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Kosc Telecom a été revendiquée le 14 juillet 2026. Le dépôt annoncé... L’article Kosc Telecom : près de 4 000 diagnostics et accès fibre dans une fuite de données est apparu en premier…

item.title: [Élevé] DoinSport : les données personnelles, adresses et profils de mineurs en fuite après une cyberattaque
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à DoinSport a été revendiquée le 14 juillet 2026. L’auteur affirme avoir récupéré... L’article DoinSport : les données personnelles, adresses et profils de mineurs en fuite après une…

item.title: [Élevé] Tech In Order : fuite de 1 400 comptes B2B, mots de passe hachés et accès administrateurs
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Tech In Order a été revendiquée le 13 juillet 2026. Le dépôt... L’article Tech In Order : fuite de 1 400 comptes B2B, mots de passe hachés et accès administrateurs est apparu en premier…

item.title: [Faible] Trump’s DNI pick grilled about election security, voter fraud
item.description: Senators pressed director of national intelligence nominee Jay Clayton about his stance on the 2020 election and previous statements about voter fraud. Other issues took a back seat.

item.title: [Élevé] 23andMe reaches $18 million settlement with states for massive breach
item.description: A coalition of 42 state attorneys general reached an $18 million settlement with 23andMe for cybersecurity failings that led to a data breach.
